sample_wo_descriptions: A5 -- SPOT CURE CRASHES (D871390C)
last_wo_description: A5 -- SPOT CURE CRASHES (D871390C)
work_performed: It was observed that there was excessive movement in the upper linkage which operates the shutter doors.  Repaired bushings.  The excessive movement was eliminated with the new bushings, but DSSA's were still hitting the shutter doors.  Adjusted set screw positioners for optimal door position but the problem was still occurring.  The rear of the station which is moved up and down by the Stelron chassis had 2 medium shims in place.  Added/removed shims (trial and error) to find the right spacing to eliminate the problem.  Ended up removing 1 medium shim and adding 1 thick shim.  The bushings that we repaired were installed in the door linkage above the top plate.  The linkage below the top plate may also need new bushings.  Was in contact with engineering throughout the troubleshooting/repair process.  Further work may be performed during December shutdown.

work_performed: Robot head #089/0113-B was on the machine.  Attempted to adjust robot head alignment -- issues got worse.  Obtained robot head #K334842 from cabinet.  Had to swap air fittings from old head to new head as the fittings provided the new head were the wrong style and, in the way, preventing it from being able to be installed.  After head #K334842 was installed, it was found that the 6th from left and 6th from right were way out of alignment.  Dis-assembled both heads.  Used bushings from head #K334842 to fix a loose spot in the scissor mechanism on head #089/0113-B.  Cleaned bearing rails and installed head #089/0113-B back into the machine.
